roadmap_checkpoints.label: HTML Basics
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn the building blocks of the web: elements, attributes, semantic markup, and document structure.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: CSS Fundamentals
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Style web pages with CSS: the box model, selectors, flexbox, and responsive design.
learning_goals: Apply responsive design with media queries

roadmap_checkpoints.label: JavaScript Basics
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Core JavaScript: variables, functions, control flow, and working with the DOM.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: DOM Manipulation
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Interact with the browser: select elements, handle events, and update the page dynamically.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: React Foundations
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Build UIs with React: components, JSX, props, state, and hooks.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Front-End Project #1
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Build a complete interactive web app using HTML, CSS, and React.

cp-003.question: Which CSS property controls the space between an element's content and its border?
cp-003.explanation: padding is the space inside the border; margin is outside.

cp-003.question: What does `display: flex` do?
options: Turns the element into a flex container
Centers the element on the page
cp-003.explanation: display: flex activates flexbox layout on the container.

cp-003.question: Which unit is relative to the viewport width?
cp-003.explanation: vw stands for viewport width — 1vw = 1% of the viewport width.

cp-003.question:
How do you select all

elements inside a .card class?
cp-003.explanation:
.card p selects any

descendant of .card, at any depth.

cp-003.question: Which media query targets screens narrower than 768px?
cp-003.explanation: max-width: 768px matches viewports up to 768px wide.

cp-006.question: What does `===` check in JavaScript?
cp-006.explanation: === is strict equality — both value and type must match.

cp-006.question: Which method adds an item to the end of an array?
cp-006.explanation: push() appends one or more items to the end of an array.

cp-006.question: What is the output of `typeof null`?
cp-006.explanation: typeof null returns 'object' — a historical JavaScript quirk.

cp-006.question: Which keyword declares a block-scoped variable?
cp-006.explanation: let and const are block-scoped; var is function-scoped.

cp-006.question: What does `Array.prototype.map()` return?
options: A new array with each element transformed
cp-006.explanation: map() returns a new array; it does not mutate the original.

learning_tasks.title: CSS Box Model — MDN
learning_tasks.description: Deep-dive into content, padding, border, and margin with interactive diagrams.

learning_tasks.title: Box Model Visualizer
learning_tasks.description: Tweak padding, margin, and border live and see the layout update instantly.
objectives: Visually grasp how each layer affects size

coding_tasks.title: Card Component Challenge
coding_tasks.description: Build a product card using box model properties — no frameworks, plain CSS.

learning_tasks.title: Flexbox Froggy
learning_tasks.description: Learn flexbox by guiding frogs to their lily pads — 24 levels of increasing difficulty.

learning_tasks.title: A Complete Guide to Flexbox — CSS-Tricks
learning_tasks.description: The canonical flexbox cheat-sheet with visual examples for every property.
objectives: All flex container and item properties

coding_tasks.title: Build a Navbar with Flexbox
coding_tasks.description: Create a responsive navigation bar: logo left, links center, CTA right.

task_resources_cache.subtopic: Apply responsive design with media queries

learning_tasks.title: Responsive Design Basics — web.dev
learning_tasks.description: Google's guide to viewport meta tags, fluid grids, and mobile-first media queries.

coding_tasks.title: Make a 3-Column Layout Responsive
coding_tasks.description: Start with a desktop 3-col grid and collapse it to a single column on mobile.

learning_tasks.title: Google Fonts + Variable Fonts Guide
learning_tasks.description: Pick, load, and optimize web fonts — plus intro to CSS custom properties for color tokens.

coding_tasks.title: Design a Typography Scale
coding_tasks.description: Define h1–h6 and body styles using CSS variables. Apply a Google Font.
